_note: visualDescription for every photography project: concrete, sensory, blind-accessible comma-separated descriptors (materials, colors, and what each surface resembles), including whether the frame reads as aerial or ground-level. winterblue/flipped/palouse already carry this in their own content-intake JSONs; merge the surface-surveys collection, its 6 chapters, and omani-landscapes into data/ssot-projects.json as a top-level `visualDescription`, rendered as image alt/longdesc for accessibility. Aerial/ground note: Greenland & Simpson = aerial; Wahiba & City of Rocks = ground-level; White Sands & Craters = both; the ambiguity of scale is intentional across the series. NOTE: field / documentary / scouting images (people, vehicles, camps, establishing shots) are NOT artworks and are excluded from these descriptions — only the abstract surface/landscape artworks are described.

visualDescription.surface-surveys: close-up earth surfaces that could be a rock held in the hand or a whole continent seen from orbit, ice, sand, lava, gypsum, granite and red dune, in blue, white, gold, black and rust, cracked, rippled, veined and crusted, the scale always ambiguous
visualDescription.greenland: glacier-white ice cut by pools of electric turquoise and deep cobalt-blue water, fine dark cracks veining the surface like shattered glass, blue meltwater rivers braiding through grey sediment-flecked ice, wet-looking and smooth, seen from far overhead like a frozen map
visualDescription.white-sands: brilliant chalk-white, like troweled and dripping wet plaster, whipped meringue and cake-frosting peaks, soft folds of melting wax and fresh snow, faint cream and pale-grey shadows, sometimes a smooth dune seen from above, sometimes a peaked crust seen up close
visualDescription.wahiba: warm honey and caramel desert sand, fine wind ripples combing the surface, scattered dark seed-pods and pebbles, delicate curling lines where wind-dragged dried grass has scribed circles and arcs into the sand, soft, dry and golden, an abstract surface where scale is hard to read
visualDescription.craters: rough volcanic rock in black and charcoal, grey stone marbled with pale swirling veins, rusty red crust blotched with mustard-yellow and lime-green lichen and orange iron stain, cracked and pitted, reading as both a boulder up close and scorched ground seen from above
visualDescription.simpson: deep brick-red and oxblood ground seen straight down from above, threaded with thin wandering vine lines that trail across it like darker-red veins, flecked with black, flat, dry and vast, like cracked red leather drawn on with fine roots
visualDescription.city-of-rocks: weathered granite up close, speckled salt-and-pepper grey, blotched with rust-orange and cream and bursts of acid-yellow and lime-green lichen, dark pockmarks and hairline cracks, rough, grainy and crusty
visualDescription.omani-landscapes: fields of scattered colored speckles in hot pink, acid green, yellow and violet, like confetti, pollen or television static, sometimes resolving into the faint silhouette of dunes or a mountain valley built entirely from grain, saturated and synthetic, a landscape dissolved into pure pattern
visualDescription.winterblue: snow-covered prairie at night in deep midnight-blue and indigo, wind-carved ridges and crusty drifts catching faint silver light, a pale haloed moon high in a dark sky, a thin seam of warm gold along the far horizon beneath black mountains, cold, still and silent
visualDescription.flipped: a row of pale blue-white icebergs with sharp jagged peaks rising from black, glass-still water, glowing as if lit from within against a dark sky, calm, silent and ghostly, ice that seems to float in darkness
visualDescription.palouse: smooth rolling farmland hills in soft waves of green and brown, fine parallel plow lines following the curves like combed corduroy, a wide, nearly empty pale sky, spare and quiet, the rounded land reading almost like a reclining body
